name: TCS (NQT / Ninja)
weightage: Aptitude-heavy: Quant 20 + Reasoning 20 + Verbal 15, then a light coding round (2 questions, basic-medium DSA) and an HR + technical combined interview.
questions: Explain your final-year project and your individual contribution to it.
Write a program to check if a string is a palindrome.
What is the difference between an array and a linked list?
How would you reverse a linked list?
Why TCS, and are you open to relocation?

name: Infosys (InfyTQ / SP)
weightage: Aptitude + Pseudocode round is decisive; coding round is 1-2 easy-medium problems; technical interview leans on OOPs and DBMS basics.
questions: What is the output of this pseudocode? (loop/array trace question)
Explain normalization with an example.
Differentiate between abstraction and encapsulation.
Write SQL to find the second-highest salary from an Employee table.
Describe a time you learned a new technology quickly for a project.

name: Wipro (Elite / Turbo)
weightage: Online assessment: aptitude + essay writing + 1-2 coding questions; technical round focuses on your resume projects and core CS basics.
questions: Walk me through the architecture of your capstone project.
What is exception handling and why is it needed?
Explain the SDLC phases briefly.
Write a function to find duplicate elements in an array.
What are your strengths that make you a good fit for Wipro?

name: Accenture
weightage: Cognitive + technical MCQ assessment, followed by a communication assessment and a combined technical-HR interview.
questions: What is the difference between a stack and a queue, with a real-world example of each?
Explain any one design pattern you have used or studied.
How do you handle tight deadlines across multiple tasks?
What is cloud computing, in your own words?
Tell me about a group project where you had to coordinate with others remotely.

name: Amazon (SDE-1)
weightage: 2 DSA-heavy online rounds (medium-hard), then 3-4 interview rounds each mapping to a Leadership Principle plus a bar-raiser round.
questions: Given an array, find the maximum sum of a contiguous subarray (Kadane's algorithm).
Design a simplified version of a URL shortener — talk through the schema and API.
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate and how you handled it (Have Backbone; Disagree and Commit).
Detect a cycle in a linked list and explain the time/space complexity.
Tell me about a time you went above and beyond what was expected of you (Bias for Action / Ownership).

name: Microsoft
weightage: Online coding round (2 medium DSA problems), then 3-4 technical interviews covering DSA, OS/DBMS fundamentals, and one project-deep-dive round.
questions: Find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes in a binary tree.
Design a data structure that supports insert, delete, and getRandom in O(1).
Explain how a hash map handles collisions.
Walk me through the most technically challenging part of a project on your resume.
What happens internally when you run a multithreaded program with a shared counter without locks?

name: Google (Early Career)
weightage: Heavy DSA online assessment (2 hard problems), followed by 2 technical (DSA + system design basics for freshers) and 1 Googleyness/leadership round.
questions: Given a matrix, find the number of islands (connected components of 1s).
Design a basic rate limiter for an API.
Explain the time and space complexity trade-offs of your solution.
Tell me about a time you had to convince a team to adopt an approach you believed in.
How would you improve a product you use daily?

name: Zoho
weightage: Written aptitude + a strong coding round (write full, compiling, correct programs on paper/editor) and a deep technical interview on fundamentals.
questions: Write a complete program (not pseudocode) to find the factorial of a number recursively.
Explain the difference between pass-by-value and pass-by-reference with an example.
What is a virtual function in C++ and why is it used?
Write a query to fetch employees who joined in the last 30 days.
How does garbage collection work in Java, broadly?
